findings.description: reachable() appends every neighbor with no 'if neighbor not in seen' visited-guard, so on any cyclic graph (which its own docstring promises to support, e.g. a->b,b->a) the BFS queue never drains, causing a non-terminating loop and unbounded deque growth — a DoS on untrusted graph input (CWE-835/CWE-400).

count_paths() recurses with no cycle guard and no memoization, so an attacker-supplied cyclic graph triggers unbounded recursion / stack exhaustion (CWE-674) while even a valid diamond-shaped DAG yields exponential-time path counting (CWE-407 algorithmic-complexity DoS); the 'DAG assumed' comment is undefended.

self._edges is a defaultdict(list) and every read of a missing key in reachable()/count_paths()/topological_order()/neighbors() (lines 30,40,47,54,61) silently inserts an empty list, so repeated queries with many distinct unknown node names grow the dict without bound — query-driven memory amplification (CWE-400); use self._edges.get(node, []) on read paths.

The module imposes no caps on graph size, recursion depth, or traversal work, providing no resource limits when fed untrusted/attacker-influenced graph data — the umbrella mitigation (CWE-770) for the count_paths/reachable DoS vectors above.
